text.title: Pain Point Research Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Pain Point Research application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Pain inventory MUST mine all four tiers: direct complaints (highest signal), questions/support, forum threads, job boards (revealed willingness to pay). Single-tier inventories are rejected.
rule.rationale: Tier triangulation eliminates confirmation bias; a single tier reflects the researcher's preferred channel.

rule.statement: Every pain MUST carry a Pain Intensity Matrix score (frequency × severity × reach × spend × alternatives, each 1-5). Unscored entries are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without quantification the log becomes opinion; PIM forces side-by-side comparability.

rule.statement: Top-10 PIM-scored pains MUST be drilled with 5 Whys to extract a root cause. Surface symptoms without root cause are rejected as actionable.
rule.rationale: Treating symptoms produces feature whack-a-mole; the root cause is the unit of work.

rule.statement: Each pain entry MUST cite ≥1 verbatim quote (with URL or transcript pointer) from the source tier. Paraphrase is rejected.
rule.rationale: Verbatim quotes are the falsifiability anchor; downstream readers can re-check the source.

rule.statement: The pain log MUST name the user segment in scope. "Generic users" is rejected.
rule.rationale: Pain priorities differ per segment; a segment-blind log is unactionable.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
